mnemonics: Universal (∀) is 'All' - think 'A' for All and upside-down 'A' for Universal. Existential (∃) is 'Exists' - think 'E' for Exists and backwards 'E' for Existential.
keyPoints: Universal Quantifier (∀x P(x)) means 'for all x, P(x) is true'.
Existential Quantifier (∃x P(x)) means 'there exists an x such that P(x) is true'.
The domain of discourse is the set from which the variable's values are chosen.
commonMistakes: Misinterpreting the scope of a quantifier, especially when negating statements.
Confusing the order of quantifiers; ∀x∃y is not logically equivalent to ∃y∀x.
